Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

returns.isTierWinner: Whether the address is a tier winner
returns.tierIndex: The 0-indexed tier (only valid if isTierWinner is true)

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

params.prizeIndex: The index in the sponsored ERC-20 array
The index in the sponsored ERC-721 array

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

params.allWinners: Flat array of winners ordered by tier (tier 0 winners first, then tier 1, etc.)

params.targetTier: The 0-indexed tier this prize is for
The 0-indexed tier this NFT goes to (first winner of that tier receives it)

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

claimSponsoredERC20(uint256).notice: Claim sponsored ERC-20 tokens for caller's tier

claimSponsoredERC721(uint256).notice: Claim sponsored ERC-721 tokens for caller's tier

configureTiers(uint256,(uint16)[]).notice: Configure tier structure for a season's sponsored prizes

getERC20TotalByToken(uint256,address).notice: Get total amount of a specific ERC-20 token sponsored for a season

getSponsoredERC20(uint256).notice: Get all sponsored ERC-20 tokens for a season

getSponsoredERC721(uint256).notice: Get all sponsored ERC-721 tokens for a season

getTierConfigs(uint256).notice: Get tier configuration for a season

getTierWinners(uint256,uint256).notice: Get winners for a specific tier

getWinnerTier(uint256,address).notice: Get which tier a winner is in

isSponsoredERC20Claimed(uint256,uint256,.notice: Check if a specific ERC-20 prize has been claimed by a winner

isSponsoredERC721Claimed(uint256,uint256.notice: Check if a specific ERC-721 prize has been claimed

lockSponsorships(uint256).notice: Lock sponsorships for a season (called when season ends)

setTierWinners(uint256,address[]).notice: Map the flat winner array from VRF to tiers

sponsorERC20(uint256,address,uint256,uin.notice: Sponsor ERC-20 tokens to a season's prize pool at a specific tier

sponsorERC721(uint256,address,uint256,ui.notice: Sponsor an ERC-721 NFT to a season's prize pool at a specific tier
